**I-5:** Status unavailable - real-time data inaccessible

**I-90 (Snoqualmie Pass):** Status unavailable - check mountain pass reports

**Highway 20 (North Cascades):** Status unavailable - real-time data inaccessible

wa-road-conditions.rawContent:
Unfortunately, I'm unable to access live road condition data from the WSDOT website. The WSDOT Travel Center pages require JavaScript to load dynamic content, which cannot be retrieved through standard web fetching tools.

**What I found:**
- The WSDOT website (wsdot.wa.gov) has a Travel Center with real-time data including mountain passes, travel alerts, cameras, and weather conditions, but it requires an interactive JavaScript-enabled browser to access
- The National Weather Service Seattle office reports some local flooding affecting rivers and roads, advising drivers to never drive around barricades

**To get current road conditions for your requested routes, I recommend:**

1. **Visit WSDOT Travel Center directly:** https://wsdot.wa.gov/travel/real-time/map (interactive map with all conditions)
2. **Call 511** (Washington State travel info hotline)
3. **Check mountain pass reports:** https://wsdot.com/travel/real-time/mountainpasses
4. **Download the WSDOT mobile app** for real-time alerts

These sources will provide current conditions for I-5, I-90 (Snoqualmie Pass), Highway 20 (North Cascades), and SR-20 to Anacortes including closures, delays, construction, and weather advisories.

Contents of https://wsdot.wa.gov/travel:
![Real-time travel data image](/sites/default/files/styles/landing_featured_section/public/2021-04/travel-realtime-feature.jpg?itok=734Nmpc7)

## [Real-time travel data](/travel/travel-data)

View up-to-date travel conditions including travel alerts, cameras, route times, mountain passes, and weather alerts.

![Commercial vehicles & trucks image](/sites/default/files/styles/landing_featured_section/public/2021-04/travel-commercialvehicles-feature.jpg?itok=f-cZII7-)

## [Commercial vehicles](/travel/commercial-vehicles)

Get over legal load permits for cars, trucks, and commercial vehicles. Learn more about commercial vehicle restrictions, route maps,…

## Learn more about...

![Curved road winding through fields and trees](/sites/default/files/styles/travel_landing_subsections/public/2021-04/roads.jpg?itok=Z5WkndY0)

### [Roads & bridges](/travel/roads-bridges)

Find details regarding HOV lanes, toll roads, updates for mountain passes and what you need to know in advance for travel around the state.

![Photo of a Washington State ferry at sunset](/sites/default/files/styles/travel_landing_subsections/public/2021-04/FerrySmall.jpg?itok=iU2ytENN)

### [Washington State Ferries](/travel/washington-state-ferries)

View sailing schedules, buy your tickets online, or save a spot for your vehicle on certain routes.

![A small plane in an air field](/sites/default/files/styles/travel_landing_subsections/public/2021-04/Aviation.jpg?itok=VtCwwY_u)

### [Aviation](/travel/aviation)

View airports, airport cameras and register your drone or aircraft.

![Commuters on bicycles heading to work next to cars on the road. ](/sites/default/files/styles/travel_landing_subsections/public/2021-08/Commuters%20bicycling%20in%20a%20large%20group%20in%20a%20bike%20lane%20next%20to%20cars%20-%20V1.jpg?itok=2NQgoRLJ)

### [Bicycling & walking](/travel/bicycling-walking)

Access walk and bike maps, rules of the road and other resources that empower you to have more transportation options.

![Photo of a Sounder train](/sites/default/files/styles/travel_landing_subsections/public/2021-04/Train.jpg?itok=pu6NhOdC)

### [Passenger rail](/travel/passenger-rail)

Find information on how we manage trains in Washington and learn about train safety and the rules to remember when traveling by passenger rail.

## [Mobile app and social media](/travel/mobile-app-and-social-media)

Stay informed with the latest travel information by downloading our mobile app and following us on social media.

## [Printable maps](/travel/printable-maps)

Get printable maps to assist with trip planning and travel.

## [Sign up for WSDOT travel alerts](/travel/sign-wsdot-travel-alerts)

There are several ways to get travel alerts from us straight to your device. Find out how to subscribe or unsubscribe to emails, push notifications or text messages. 

## [Holiday weekend travel](/travel/holiday-weekend-travel)

View holiday travel charts for the four routes that historically see the most holiday traffic congestion and do not have other alternative route options.

## [Traffic safety methods](/travel/traffic-safety-methods)

Find details about the methods we use to keep travelers safe, including roundabouts, road equipment and striping, and workzone safety information.

## [Operations & services](/travel/operations-services)

Learn about the tools we use to reduce traffic congestion and collisions, including ramp meters, traffic management centers, traffic signals and our incident response team.

## [Drive safe in winter weather](https://wsdot.com/travel/real-time/mountainpasses/winterdrivingtips)

### Slow down on ice and snow.

It's easier to skid or lose control traveling at higher speeds. Give yourself more time to stop.

### Carry chains, practice installing them.

Winter conditions could mean chains are required on your route. Practice putting them on your vehicle ahead of time.

### Pack your winter car kit.

Carry extra supplies like warm clothing, ice scraper and brush, jumper cables and other emergency items.

## Heat the Southwest; Fire Weather in the Central U.S.; Kona Low Impacting Hawaii

A rare March heat wave is ongoing with much above-normal temperatures over the Southwest U.S. through this weekend. Periods of critical fire weather will persist from the central Rockies to the central and southern Plains through the weekend as gusty winds and low relative humidity continue. A Kona low will continue to bring several rounds of moderate to heavy rainfall to Hawaii through Sunday. [Read More >](http://www.wpc.ncep.noaa.gov/discussions/hpcdiscussions.php?disc=pmdspd)

**Tomorrow's Forecast for Seattle, Washington (March 22, 2026):**

**Morning:** Partly cloudy with clearing skies. Temperatures starting at 42°F with light south-southeast winds.

**Afternoon:** Clear to partly cloudy conditions as temperatures warm to a high of 48°F. Dry conditions continue with calm winds at 1-5 mph.

**Evening:** Temperatures cooling to the low of 38°F under mostly clear skies.

**Overall:** A dry, cool spring day with 0% chance of rain throughout. Perfect conditions for outdoor activities.

**OVERALL RECOMMENDATION: FAIR TRAVEL DAY** – Excellent weather conditions but mandatory road verification required due to regional flooding.

**WEATHER OUTLOOK – OPTIMAL**
Seattle to Orcas Island ferry crossing on March 22 features ideal conditions: sunny skies, 42-49°F temperatures, 0% precipitation, and light westerly winds (6 mph) transitioning to calm. Ferry crossing conditions are excellent.

**CRITICAL CONCERNS**
Real-time road data shows active flooding advisory affecting I-5 North and SR-20 routes to Anacortes. WSDOT verification is **mandatory** before departure. Do not proceed without confirming road accessibility via wsdot.wa.gov/travel or calling 511. Never drive around flood barricades.

**RECOMMENDED DEPARTURE**
Morning departure (8-10 AM) allows time for road condition verification, takes advantage of warming temperatures, and ensures daylight contingency options. Confirm ferry reservation and consider pre-booking.

**ESSENTIAL PREPARATIONS**
- Layer clothing for 39-49°F range
- Light jacket and sun protection for ferry crossing
- Full fuel tank
- Emergency kit (blanket, water, snacks)
- Download WSDOT app for real-time alerts

**BOTTOM LINE**
Weather conditions are perfect for island travel, but flooding concerns elevate this from "good" to "fair" travel day. Plan to spend 15-20 minutes verifying road conditions before departure. Once roads are confirmed clear, expect a smooth, scenic journey with ideal ferry crossing conditions.

# TRAVEL ADVISORY: Seattle to Orcas Island
**Travel Date:** March 22, 2026 (Tomorrow, Saturday)

---

## 1) CURRENT CONDITIONS SUMMARY (March 21)
- **Seattle:** Overcast, 47°F, light winds - typical early spring weather
- No immediate concerns for tomorrow's preparation

---

## 2) TOMORROW'S TRAVEL OUTLOOK ⭐ FAVORABLE

**Weather:**
- **Seattle:** Clear to partly cloudy, 42-48°F, 0% rain, calm winds
- **Orcas Island:** Sunny, 49°F high, 0% rain through end of day
- **Ferry Crossing:** Optimal conditions with light westerly winds (6 mph) becoming calm

**Road Conditions:** ⚠️ **VERIFICATION REQUIRED**
- **CRITICAL:** Real-time road data unavailable for I-5 and SR-20 to Anacortes
- **ALERT:** Local flooding reported by NWS affecting area rivers/roads
- **Action Required:** Check WSDOT.wa.gov/travel before departure

---

## 3) RECOMMENDED DEPARTURE TIME

**Morning Departure (8-10 AM):** Best option
- Allows time to verify road conditions
- Takes advantage of warming temps (42°F → 48°F)
- Ensures daylight return if needed
- Ferry availability typically better mid-morning

---

The [National Weather Service (NWS) Marine Weather Services Program](https://weather.gov/marine/mttservices) offers a broad range of marine forecast and warning products in [graphical](https://ocean.weather.gov/) and [text](https://www.weather.gov/marine/marinewxi) (low bandwidth) formats (See Tabs above). The NWS provides forecasts and warning services for the [coastal waters](https://www.weather.gov/marine/usamz) along the mainland of the continental U.S., the [Great Lakes](https://www.weather.gov/greatlakes/) and the [Offshore](https://www.weather.gov/marine/wrdoffmz) and [High Seas](https://www.weather.gov/marine/hsmz) waters of the North Atlantic and North Pacific Oceans. Links to forecasts, warnings and products related to [tropical cyclones](https://www.nhc.noaa.gov/cyclones/) and [sea ice](https://usicecenter.gov/) are near the bottom of the page. The program also provides important [Tsunami](https://tsunami.gov/) information. You can also get an [**hourly** marine forecast for a single point](https://www.weather.gov/marine/point) and marine channel forecasts for [Tampa, FL](https://tidesandcurrents.noaa.gov/ofs/tbofs/tbmcf.html) and [Mobile Bay, AL](https://tidesandcurrents.noaa.gov/ofs/ngofs2/mbmcf.html). Read our [blog](https://www.weather.gov/marine/blog) for important changes and news within the NWS marine, tropical and tsunami programs. 

Click [here](https://www.weather.gov/gis/MarineZones) for the latest maps of official NWS marine forecast and warning zones (includes any recent changes to coastal, offshore and high seas zones). 

Clicking on an area of interest on the map below will take you to marine webpages of Weather Forecast Offices (WFOs) and to a web portal for the Great Lakes. Here is a list of [NWS offices by region.](https://www.weather.gov/srh/nwsoffices) Click here for [m](https://www.weather.gov/marine/AllZones)[aps showing all NWS marine forecast and warning zones](https://weather.gov/marine/AllZones)[.](https://www.weather.gov/marine/AllZones)

## [Real-time travel data](/travel/travel-data)

View up-to-date travel conditions including travel alerts, cameras, route times, mountain passes, and weather alerts.

## [Commercial vehicles](/travel/commercial-vehicles)

Get over legal load permits for cars, trucks, and commercial vehicles. Learn more about commercial vehicle restrictions, route maps,…

## Learn more about...

### [Roads & bridges](/travel/roads-bridges)

Find details regarding HOV lanes, toll roads, updates for mountain passes and what you need to know in advance for travel around the state.

### [Washington State Ferries](/travel/washington-state-ferries)

View sailing schedules, buy your tickets online, or save a spot for your vehicle on certain routes.

### [Aviation](/travel/aviation)

View airports, airport cameras and register your drone or aircraft.

![Commuters on bicycles heading to work next to cars on the road. ](/sites/default/files/styles/travel_landing_subsections/public/2021-08/Commuters%20bicycling%20in%20a%20large%20group%20in%20a%20bike%20lane%20next%20to%20cars%20-%20V1.jpg?itok=2NQgoRLJ)

### [Bicycling & walking](/travel/bicycling-walking)

Access walk and bike maps, rules of the road and other resources that empower you to have more transportation options.

### [Passenger rail](/travel/passenger-rail)

Find information on how we manage trains in Washington and learn about train safety and the rules to remember when traveling by passenger rail.

## [Mobile app and social media](/travel/mobile-app-and-social-media)

Stay informed with the latest travel information by downloading our mobile app and following us on social media.

## [Printable maps](/travel/printable-maps)

Get printable maps to assist with trip planning and travel.

## [Sign up for WSDOT travel alerts](/travel/sign-wsdot-travel-alerts)

There are several ways to get travel alerts from us straight to your device. Find out how to subscribe or unsubscribe to emails, push notifications or text messages. 

## [Holiday weekend travel](/travel/holiday-weekend-travel)

View holiday travel charts for the four routes that historically see the most holiday traffic congestion and do not have other alternative route options.

## [Traffic safety methods](/travel/traffic-safety-methods)

Find details about the methods we use to keep travelers safe, including roundabouts, road equipment and striping, and workzone safety information.

## [Operations & services](/travel/operations-services)

Learn about the tools we use to reduce traffic congestion and collisions, including ramp meters, traffic management centers, traffic signals and our incident response team.

## [Drive safe in winter weather](https://wsdot.com/travel/real-time/mountainpasses/winterdrivingtips)

### Slow down on ice and snow.

It's easier to skid or lose control traveling at higher speeds. Give yourself more time to stop.

### Carry chains, practice installing them.

Winter conditions could mean chains are required on your route. Practice putting them on your vehicle ahead of time.

### Pack your winter car kit.

Carry extra supplies like warm clothing, ice scraper and brush, jumper cables and other emergency items.

- The WSDOT website (wsdot.wa.gov) has a Travel Center with real-time data including mountain passes, travel alerts, cameras, and weather conditions, but it requires an interactive JavaScript-enabled browser to access

**To get current road conditions for your requested routes, I recommend:**

1. **Visit WSDOT Travel Center directly:** https://wsdot.wa.gov/travel/real-time/map (interactive map with all conditions)

3. **Check mountain pass reports:** https://wsdot.com/travel/real-time/mountainpasses

**OVERALL RECOMMENDATION: FAIR TRAVEL DAY** – Excellent weather conditions but mandatory road verification required due to regional flooding.

**WEATHER OUTLOOK – OPTIMAL**
Seattle to Orcas Island ferry crossing on March 22 features ideal conditions: sunny skies, 42-49°F temperatures, 0% precipitation, and light westerly winds (6 mph) transitioning to calm. Ferry crossing conditions are excellent.

**CRITICAL CONCERNS**
Real-time road data shows active flooding advisory affecting I-5 North and SR-20 routes to Anacortes. WSDOT verification is **mandatory** before departure. Do not proceed without confirming road accessibility via wsdot.wa.gov/travel or calling 511. Never drive around flood barricades.

**RECOMMENDED DEPARTURE**
Morning departure (8-10 AM) allows time for road condition verification, takes advantage of warming temperatures, and ensures daylight contingency options. Confirm ferry reservation and consider pre-booking.

**ESSENTIAL PREPARATIONS**
- Layer clothing for 39-49°F range
- Light jacket and sun protection for ferry crossing
- Full fuel tank
- Emergency kit (blanket, water, snacks)
- Download WSDOT app for real-time alerts

**BOTTOM LINE**
Weather conditions are perfect for island travel, but flooding concerns elevate this from "good" to "fair" travel day. Plan to spend 15-20 minutes verifying road conditions before departure. Once roads are confirmed clear, expect a smooth, scenic journey with ideal ferry crossing conditions.
